title: Configure VLAN and Access Ports
scenario: A switch supports Sales and Engineering departments. Segment users into VLAN 10 and VLAN 20.
topology: 1 switch, 4 PCs. PC1/PC2 on fa0/1-2 (Sales), PC3/PC4 on fa0/3-4 (Engineering).
steps: Create VLANs with clear names.
Assign access ports to each VLAN.
Verify with show vlan brief.
verification: show vlan brief and test L2 connectivity only inside same VLAN.

title: Configure 802.1Q Trunking
scenario: Two switches must carry VLAN 10 and VLAN 20 across an uplink.
topology: SW1 g0/1 <-> SW2 g0/1 trunk link, users in both VLANs on each switch.
steps: Set both uplink interfaces to trunk mode.
Allow required VLANs only.
Verify trunk operational state.
verification: show interfaces trunk and cross-switch ping in same VLAN.

title: Configure Static Routing
scenario: Connect two LANs through two routers using static routes.
steps: Address all interfaces and bring them up.
Configure static routes on both routers.
Test end-to-end ping and traceroute.
verification: show ip route and successful ping between LAN A and LAN B hosts.

title: Configure Single-Area OSPF
scenario: Three routers in area 0 must exchange routes dynamically.
topology: R1-R2-R3 triangle, each with local loopback network.
steps: Enable OSPF process on each router.
Advertise links and loopbacks in area 0.
Verify neighbor adjacency and learned routes.
verification: show ip ospf neighbor and show ip route ospf.

title: Configure Extended ACL
scenario: Allow only HTTP from VLAN 10 to server 172.16.1.10; block all else.
topology: VLAN 10 users -> Router -> Server network
steps: Build ACL entries in correct order.
Apply ACL inbound on source-facing interface.
Validate with allowed and denied traffic tests.
verification: show access-lists and packet counters increase on matching lines.

title: Configure PAT (NAT Overload)
scenario: Branch users need internet access through one ISP address.
requiredCommands: ip nat inside source list 1 interface g0/0 overload
steps: Define inside network ACL.
Mark inside and outside interfaces.
Apply PAT rule and verify translations.
verification: show ip nat translations while hosts generate outbound traffic.

title: Configure DHCP Service
scenario: Router must assign addresses to office clients in VLAN 50.
topology: Router-on-a-stick gateway for VLAN 50, clients on access switch.
steps: Exclude reserved static range.
Create DHCP pool with network and default gateway.
Renew client leases and verify bindings.
verification: show ip dhcp binding and client obtains expected network settings.

title: Configure VLAN Access Ports 10/20
scenario: Segment Sales into VLAN 10 and Finance into VLAN 20.
topology: 1 switch, 4 PCs. Sales on fa0/1-2, Finance on fa0/3-4.
steps: Create both VLANs with clear names.
Assign access ports to the correct VLAN.
Verify with show vlan brief.
verification: show vlan brief shows correct port membership.
